perturbation.original_text: 5. DURATION This agreement shall commence on the date of signat ure of this agreement by the last party to sign the agreement and shall remain in force indefinitel y thereafter (and, in any event, beyond the term of this agreement).
perturbation.changed_text: 5. DURATION This agreement shall commence on the date of signature of this agreement by the last party to sign the agreement and shall remain in force for a period of thirty (30) days thereafter (and, in any event, beyond the term of this agreement).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the agreement remains in force for a period of 30 days after signing, conflicting with the original statement that it remains in force indefinitely. This creates uncertainty regarding how long the NDA remains in effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. DURATION This agreement shall commence on the date of signature of this agreement by the last party to sign the agreement and shall remain in force for a period of thirty (30) days thereafter (and, in any event, beyond the term of this agreement).
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement remains in force indefinitely. The changed text modifies this to state the agreement remains in force for 30 days. This directly contradicts the original intent of indefinite duration and creates a clear inconsistency regarding the duration of the agreement, and the change text is exactly the same as the contradicted text, resulting in a direct contradiction and making it difficult to determine how long the agreement should be.

perturbation.original_text: 7. ENFORCEMENT, GOVERNING LAWS AND JURISDICTION 7.1 This agreement shall be governed by and interpr eted according to the laws of the Republic of South Africa, without reference to the choice of laws' provisions of the Republic of South Africa. 7.2 7.3 The Parties irrevocably submit to the non-exclu sive jurisdiction of the High Court of South Africa, Gauteng Division, in respect of an y action or proceeding arising from this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. ENFORCEMENT, GOVERNING LAWS AND JURISDICTION 7.1 This agreement shall be governed by and interpreted according to the laws of the State of Delaware, without reference to the choice of laws' provisions of the Republic of South Africa. 7.2 7.3 The Parties irrevocably submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the High Court of South Africa, Gauteng Division, in respect of any action or proceeding arising from this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change to section 7.1 introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, the agreement was governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa. The modification now states that it is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware. This is contradictory, as both cannot govern the agreement simultaneously. While the jurisdiction remains in South Africa, the applicable laws are now in conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. ENFORCEMENT, GOVERNING LAWS AND JURISDICTION 7.1 This agreement shall be governed by and interpreted according to the laws of the State of Delaware, without reference to the choice of laws' provisions of the Republic of South Africa. 7.2 7.3 The Parties irrevocably submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the High Court of South Africa, Gauteng Division, in respect of any action or proceeding arising from this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 7.1 from governing laws of the Republic of South Africa to the State of Delaware. The contradicted text is the exact same, but that is not an indicator that there is no contradiction. According to the explanation, this change introduces an in-text contradiction because the agreement cannot simultaneously be governed by the laws of two different jurisdictions, making the terms inconsistent and therefore contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: 9.2 No addition to, variation or agreed cancellatio n of this agreement shall be of any force or effect unless in writing and signed by or on behalf of the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.2 No addition to, variation or agreed cancellation of this agreement shall be of any force or effect unless communicated verbally between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by changing the requirement for modifications to be in writing and signed to a requirement for modifications to be communicated verbally. This directly clashes with the original clause, causing ambiguity about how the agreement can be legally amended. It undermines the original intention of formalizing any changes in writing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.2 No addition to, variation or agreed cancellation of this agreement shall be of any force or effect unless communicated verbally between the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that modifications to the agreement must be in writing and signed. The changed text states that modifications must be communicated verbally. The contradicted text restates this changed text. The changed text directly contradicts the original clause of requiring written and signed modifications, by requiring verbal communication instead, leading to a clear in-text contradiction. This is an 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', as defined because it introduces conflicting requirements for how the agreement can be amended.
